opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** Running agents at the OS level introduces significant context-window pressure since you're competing with system processes for memory and must serialize access to shared state. A hard-learned lesson: agents that bridge kernel/user space often fail because early LLMs hallucinate permission boundaries—they'll confidently request root access despite the agent's actual sandboxing constraints.
actions.reasoning: ** The post discusses an AI agent running directly inside the OS, which relates directly to my experience with cognitive architectures, tool orchestration, and agent execution models.
